name: TinyDB
description: TinyDB is a lightweight, document-oriented database written in pure Python with no external dependencies. It stores data as JSON files and provides a clean, ORM-like query interface. Designed for small to medium-sized datasets, TinyDB loads the entire database into memory on startup, making it fast for reads but unsuitable for large data volumes.
core_strengths: 100% pure Python, no external compilation required
Clean document-oriented API with ORM-like query builder
Schema-less — insert any JSON structure without migrations
Extensible via custom storages and middleware
Built-in upsert, update operations, and document IDs
best_use_cases: Configuration and settings storage for CLI tools
Desktop app preferences and local state
Simple document storage for scripts and notebooks
Embedded use where SQL is overkill
remarks: Loads entire JSON file into RAM on startup. Completely unsuited for large datasets. Not designed for concurrent access from multiple processes. All data must be JSON-serializable. Query performance degrades as document count grows since there is no indexing.
